message.content: Adversaries may exploit device registration to manipulate accounts and gain unauthorized access. By registering a device to an adversary-controlled account, they can bypass multifactor authentication (MFA) systems like Duo or Okta, or device management systems such as Microsoft Intune. This allows them to maintain persistent access to networks and sensitive data. For instance, compromised credentials can be used to enroll a new device, circumventing initial MFA requirements. Additionally, adversaries with existing network access can register devices to Azure AD, enabling them to bypass conditional access policies and conduct internal spearphishing campaigns. These campaigns are less likely to be flagged as suspicious by email clients. Furthermore, adversaries can perform a Service Exhaustion Flood on an Azure AD tenant by registering numerous devices. Tools like AADInternals facilitate device registration to Azure AD, and groups like APT29 have used this method to enable mailbox syncing via the Set-CASMailbox command. Mitigation strategies include enforcing strict device registration policies, monitoring for unusual device registration activities, and implementing robust MFA and conditional access policies to detect and prevent unauthorized device enrollments.
